spec_match.rationale: Provides a highly thorough and compliant test of light-dark() in multiple list styling slots, including list-style-image directly, and content / background-image on pseudo-elements.

interactive.rationale: Simple and reliable theme toggle buttons that update color-scheme through root class modifiers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Parent blurb matches the list style bullets concept perfectly.

edge_cases.rationale: Outstanding coverage: tests checkmarks, arrows, counter-based badges, and status-specific icons, all driven by light-dark() image swaps.

references.rationale: The page completely lacks the mandatory ChromeStatus reference link in its see also section.

design_system.rationale: Strong adherence to the design system, utilizing clear spacing and proper typography.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the required reference link to `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5123253146353664`. It should be added to the 'see also' section to conform to the repository rules.

summary: An exceptionally comprehensive demo demonstrating four different ways to style lists using light-dark() with SVG data URIs. It is highly interactive, but fails to include the mandatory ChromeStatus reference link.
